Food & Culture

Taste the flavours and traditions of Afghanistan

Book a Tour
Cuisine & Culture

Afghan Food & Traditions

From fragrant rice dishes to seasonal produce — Afghan cuisine reflects the country's diverse landscape and rich history.

Beverage Bread Dessert Main Produce Snack
Main Dinner

Kabuli Pulao

Afghanistan's national rice dish with lamb carrots and raisins.

Main Dinner

Ghazni Pulao

Rich variation of Kabuli pulao known for deep flavor.

Main Dinner

Kandahar Rosht

Slow cooked lamb traditionally served with naan.

Main Dinner

Qaroot Khurti

Hazaragi yogurt stew with mint and garlic.

Main Lunch

Korma e Herati

Herat style herb stew with tender meat.

Main Lunch

Aushak Dumplings

Leek dumplings topped with yogurt and meat sauce.

Main Lunch

Mantu Dumplings

Steamed dumplings filled with minced meat.

Main Dinner

Chapli Kebab

Spiced minced beef kebab popular across Afghanistan.

Main Dinner

Herati Kebab

Grilled lamb kebab seasoned with herbs.

Bread Side

Afghan Naan

Traditional long flatbread baked in clay ovens.

Bread Snack

Bolani Flatbread

Pan fried flatbread stuffed with potatoes or herbs.

Snack Tea-Time

Sambosa

Fried pastry filled with meat or vegetables.

Dessert Snack

Sheer e Yakh

Rose water ice cream topped with clotted cream.

Dessert Tea-Time

Sheer Pira

Milk fudge flavored with cardamom.

Dessert Tea-Time

Gosh e Fil

Fried elephant ear pastry served with sugar.

Dessert Tea-Time

Firni

Creamy milk pudding flavored with cardamom.

Dessert Tea-Time

Halwa Afghan

Traditional sweet made with flour sugar and nuts.

Beverage Tea-Time

Afghan Green Tea

Traditional green tea served after meals.

Beverage Tea-Time

Saffron Tea

Tea infused with Herat saffron strands.

Beverage Tea-Time

Kandahar Milk Tea

Black tea served with milk and sugar.

Produce Side

Bamyan Potatoes

Buttery potatoes grown in Bamyan valley.

Produce Snack

Wardak Apples

Export quality apples from Wardak orchards.

Produce Snack

Kandahar Pomegranate

Ruby red pomegranates famous across Afghanistan.

Produce Spice

Herat Saffron

Premium saffron grown near Herat.

Produce Snack

Balkh Melon

Sweet melon grown in Balkh province.

Produce Snack

Kunduz Melon

Northern summer melon with floral aroma.

Produce Snack

Panjshir Walnuts

Fresh walnuts harvested in Panjshir valley.

Produce Snack

Farah Watermelon

Large red watermelon grown in Farah.

Produce Snack

Ghor Grapes

Mountain grapes grown in Ghor.

Produce Snack

Jowzjan Gorgak Melon

Small sweet melon grown by Turkmen farmers.

Main Dinner

Kabul Kebab

Street grilled kebab served with naan.

Main Dinner

Qabili Chicken

Chicken variation of Kabuli pulao.

Main Dinner

Herati Pilaf

Rice dish seasoned with saffron.

Main Dinner

Kandahar Kebab

Lamb kebab cooked over charcoal.

Main Lunch

Ash Soup

Thick noodle soup with herbs.

Main Lunch

Shorwa Soup

Traditional Afghan meat broth soup.

Beverage Snack

Balkh Yogurt Drink

Salted yogurt drink served cold.

Beverage Snack

Lassi Drink

Yogurt drink popular in summer.

Dessert Snack

Herat Ice Cream

Saffron flavored Afghan ice cream.

Snack Snack

Dried Fruits Mix

Traditional mix of raisins almonds pistachios.

Produce Snack

Pistachios Samangan

Famous wild pistachios from Samangan.

Produce Snack

Herat Raisins

Sun dried raisins from Herat vineyards.

Produce Snack

Balkh Almonds

Crunchy almonds grown in Balkh province.

Bread Side

Sheermal Bread

Slightly sweet saffron flavored bread.

Dessert Tea-Time

Kulcha Cookie

Traditional Afghan tea cookie.

Dessert Tea-Time

Jalebi Sweet

Deep fried syrup soaked spiral sweet.

Dessert Tea-Time

Herat Baklava

Layered pastry with nuts and honey.

Dessert Tea-Time

Kandahar Sweets

Regional sweet pastries served with tea.

Discover the breathtaking landscapes, ancient history, and rich culture of Afghanistan with our expert-guided tours.
